revision.text:
{{Short description|Archaeological site in the Tōkai region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Wadaoka Kofun group | native_name = 和田岡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Wadaoka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|40|N|137|57|06|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kakegawa, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Wadaoka Kofun group'''|和田岡古墳群|Wadaoka kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] with a cluster of [[burial mound]]s from the [[Kofun period]] located in what is now part of the city of [[Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1996.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138687|title=和田岡古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The Wadaoka Kofun group is located in an area measuring approximately 2.5 kilometers north-south by one kilometer east-west, on a low plateau overlooking the Haranoya River west of the downtown area of modern Kakegawa city. The site consists of four [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}), 15 {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), and four {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped ''kofun''|ja|方墳}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳}}), all dating from the 5th century to the sixth century AD. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The largest include: {| class="wikitable" |+ Wadaoka Kofun group |- ! Name !! Type !! Length |- | Wakanezuka Kofun
各和金塚古墳 || keyhole || 66.4 meters
Anterior: 20.5 m wide x 4 m high: Posterior 51.2 m diameter x 6.5 m high |- | Hisagozuka Kofun
瓢塚古墳 || keyhole || 63 meters long
Anterior: 25.2 m wide x 3.5 m high: Posterior 37.8 m diameter x 3.5 m high |- | Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun
吉岡大塚古墳|| keyhole || 55 meters long
Anterior: 27.5 m wide x 2.5 m high: Posterior 41.3 m diameter x 7.2 m high |- | Gyojinzuka Kofun
行人塚古墳 || keyhole || 43.7 meters long
Anterior: 16 m wide: Posterior 25.4 m diameter |- | Harubayashiin Kofun
春林院古墳 || circular || 35 meters, height 5 meters |- | Usa-Hachiman-sha Kofun || circular || 30 meter diameter |- | Gongenyama Kofun || circular || 40 meter diameter |- | Ishinogata Kofun || circular || 25 meter diameter |- |} These tumuli are believed to be the tombs of local kings who ruled the Haranodani River basin. The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. [[Grave goods]] recovered from the tombs included, large numbers of iron weapons, fragments of armor, pottery shards and ''[[shinju-kyo]]'' [[bronze mirrors]]. The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s [[Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|Hosoya Station]]. There are plans to make an [[archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]]

{{Short description|Archaeological site in the Tōkai region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Wadaoka Kofun group | native_name = 和田岡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Wadaoka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|40|N|137|57|06|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kakegawa, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Wadaoka Kofun group'''|和田岡古墳群|Wadaoka kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] with a cluster of [[burial mound]]s from the [[Kofun period]] located in what is now part of the city of [[Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1996.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138687|title=和田岡古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The Wadaoka Kofun group is located in an area measuring approximately 2.5 kilometers north-south by one kilometer east-west, on a low plateau overlooking the Haranoya River west of the downtown area of modern Kakegawa city. The site consists of four [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}), 15 {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), and four {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped ''kofun''|ja|方墳}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳}}), all dating from the 5th century to the sixth century AD. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The largest include: {| class="wikitable" |+ Wadaoka Kofun group |- ! name !! Type !! Length |- | Wakanezuka Kofun
各和金塚古墳 || keyhole || 66.4 meters
Anterior: 20.5 m wide x 4 m high: Posterior 51.2 m diameter x 6.5 m high |- | Hisagozuka Kofun
瓢塚古墳 || keyhole || 63 meters long
Anterior: 25.2 m wide x 3.5 m high: Posterior 37.8 m diameter x 3.5 m high |- | Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun
吉岡大塚古墳|| keyhole || 55 meters long
Anterior: 27.5 m wide x 2.5 m high: Posterior 41.3 m diameter x 7.2 m high |- | Gyojinzuka Kofun
行人塚古墳 || keyhole || 43.7 meters long
Anterior: 16 m wide: Posterior 25.4 m diameter |- | Harubayashiin Kofun
春林院古墳 || circular || 35 meters, height 5 meters |- | Usa-Hachiman-sha Kofun || circular || 30 meter diameter |- | Gongenyama Kofun || circular || 40 meter diameter |- | Ishinogata Kofun || circular || 25 meter diameter |- |} These tumuli are believed to be the tombs of local kings who ruled the Haranodani River basin. The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. [[Grave goods]] recovered from the tombs included, large numbers of iron weapons, fragments of armor, pottery shards and ''[[shinju-kyo]]'' [[bronze mirrors]]. The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s [[Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|Hosoya Station]]. There are plans to make an [[archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]]

The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. [[Grave goods]] recovered from the tombs included, large numbers of iron weapons, fragments of armor, pottery shards and ''[[shinju-kyo]]'' [[bronze mirrors]]. The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s [[Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|Hosoya Station]]. There are plans to make an [[archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]]

The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s [[Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|Hosoya Station]]. {{Short description|Archaeological site in the Tōkai region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Wadaoka Kofun group | native_name = 和田岡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Wadaoka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|40|N|137|57|06|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kakegawa, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Wadaoka Kofun group'''|和田岡古墳群|Wadaoka kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] with a cluster of [[burial mound]]s from the [[Kofun period]] located in what is now part of the city of [[Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1996.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138687|title=和田岡古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The Wadaoka Kofun group is located in an area measuring approximately 2.5 kilometers north-south by one kilometer east-west, on a low plateau overlooking the Haranoya River west of the downtown area of modern Kakegawa city. The site consists of four [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}), 15 {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), and four {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped ''kofun''|ja|方墳}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳}}), all dating from the 5th century to the sixth century AD. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The largest include: {| class="wikitable" |+ Wadaoka Kofun group |- ! name !! Type !! Length |- | Wakanezuka Kofun
各和金塚古墳 || keyhole || 66.4 meters
Anterior: 20.5 m wide x 4 m high: Posterior 51.2 m diameter x 6.5 m high |- | Hisagozuka Kofun
瓢塚古墳 || keyhole || 63 meters long
Anterior: 25.2 m wide x 3.5 m high: Posterior 37.8 m diameter x 3.5 m high |- | Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun
吉岡大塚古墳|| keyhole || 55 meters long
Anterior: 27.5 m wide x 2.5 m high: Posterior 41.3 m diameter x 7.2 m high |- | Gyojinzuka Kofun
行人塚古墳 || keyhole || 43.7 meters long
Anterior: 16 m wide: Posterior 25.4 m diameter |- | Harubayashiin Kofun
春林院古墳 || circular || 35 meters, height 5 meters |- | Usa-Hachiman-sha Kofun || circular || 30 meter diameter |- | Gongenyama Kofun || circular || 40 meter diameter |- | Ishinogata Kofun || circular || 25 meter diameter |- |} These tumuli are believed to be the tombs of local kings who ruled the Haranodani River basin. The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. [[Grave goods]] recovered from the tombs included, large numbers of iron weapons, fragments of armor, pottery shards and ''[[shinju-kyo]]'' [[bronze mirrors]]. The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s [[Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|Hosoya Station]]. There are plans to make an [[archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]]

{{Short description|Archaeological site in the Tōkai region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Wadaoka Kofun group | native_name = 和田岡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Wadaoka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|40|N|137|57|06|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kakegawa, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Wadaoka Kofun group'''|和田岡古墳群|Wadaoka kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] with a cluster of [[burial mound]]s from the [[Kofun period]] located in what is now part of the city of [[Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1996.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138687|title=和田岡古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The Wadaoka Kofun group is located in an area measuring approximately 2.5 kilometers north-south by one kilometer east-west, on a low plateau overlooking the Haranoya River west of the downtown area of modern Kakegawa city. The site consists of four [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}), 15 {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), and four {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped ''kofun''|ja|方墳}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳}}), all dating from the 5th century to the sixth century AD. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The largest include: {| class="wikitable" |+ Wadaoka Kofun group |- ! name !! Type !! Length |- | Wakanezuka Kofun
各和金塚古墳 || keyhole || 66.4 meters
Anterior: 20.5 m wide x 4 m high: Posterior 51.2 m diameter x 6.5 m high |- | Hisagozuka Kofun
瓢塚古墳 || keyhole || 63 meters long
Anterior: 25.2 m wide x 3.5 m high: Posterior 37.8 m diameter x 3.5 m high |- | Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun
吉岡大塚古墳|| keyhole || 55 meters long
Anterior: 27.5 m wide x 2.5 m high: Posterior 41.3 m diameter x 7.2 m high |- | Gyojinzuka Kofun
行人塚古墳 || keyhole || 43.7 meters long
Anterior: 16 m wide: Posterior 25.4 m diameter |- | Harubayashiin Kofun
春林院古墳 || circular || 35 meters, height 5 meters |- | Usa-Hachiman-sha Kofun || circular || 30 meter diameter |- | Gongenyama Kofun || circular || 40 meter diameter |- | Ishinogata Kofun || circular || 25 meter diameter |- |} These tumuli are believed to be the tombs of local kings who ruled the Haranodani River basin. The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. [[Grave goods]] recovered from the tombs included, large numbers of iron weapons, fragments of armor, pottery shards and ''[[shinju-kyo]]'' [[bronze mirrors]]. The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s [[Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|Hosoya Station]]. There are plans to make an [[archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:円墳]]

There are plans to make an [[archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:円墳]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Archaeological site in the Tōkai region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Wadaoka Kofun group | native_name = 和田岡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Wadaoka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|40|N|137|57|06|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kakegawa, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Wadaoka Kofun group'''|和田岡古墳群|Wadaoka kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] with a cluster of [[burial mound]]s from the [[Kofun period]] located in what is now part of the city of [[Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1996.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138687|title=和田岡古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The Wadaoka Kofun group is located in an area measuring approximately 2.5 kilometers north-south by one kilometer east-west, on a low plateau overlooking the Haranoya River west of the downtown area of modern Kakegawa city. The site consists of four [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}), 15 {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), and four {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped ''kofun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), all dating from the 5th century to the sixth century AD. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The largest include: {| class="wikitable" |+ Wadaoka Kofun group |- ! name !! Type !! Length |- | Wakanezuka Kofun
各和金塚古墳 || keyhole || 66.4 meters
Anterior: 20.5 m wide x 4 m high: Posterior 51.2 m diameter x 6.5 m high |- | Hisagozuka Kofun
瓢塚古墳 || keyhole || 63 meters long
Anterior: 25.2 m wide x 3.5 m high: Posterior 37.8 m diameter x 3.5 m high |- | Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun
吉岡大塚古墳|| keyhole || 55 meters long
Anterior: 27.5 m wide x 2.5 m high: Posterior 41.3 m diameter x 7.2 m high |- | Gyojinzuka Kofun
行人塚古墳 || keyhole || 43.7 meters long
Anterior: 16 m wide: Posterior 25.4 m diameter |- | Harubayashiin Kofun
春林院古墳 || circular || 35 meters, height 5 meters |- | Usa-Hachiman-sha Kofun || circular || 30 meter diameter |- | Gongenyama Kofun || circular || 40 meter diameter |- | Ishinogata Kofun || circular || 25 meter diameter |- |} These tumuli are believed to be the tombs of local kings who ruled the Haranodani River basin. The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. [[Grave goods]] recovered from the tombs included, large numbers of iron weapons, fragments of armor, pottery shards and ''[[shinju-kyo]]'' [[bronze mirrors]]. The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s [[Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|Hosoya Station]]. There are plans to make an [[archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:前方後円墳]]

春林院古墳 || circular || 35 meters, height 5 meters |- | Usa-Hachiman-sha Kofun || circular || 30 meter diameter |- | Gongenyama Kofun || circular || 40 meter diameter |- | Ishinogata Kofun || circular || 25 meter diameter |- |} These tumuli are believed to be the tombs of local kings who ruled the Haranodani River basin. The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. [[Grave goods]] recovered from the tombs included, large numbers of iron weapons, fragments of armor, pottery shards and ''[[shinju-kyo]]'' [[bronze mirrors]]. The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s [[Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|Hosoya Station]]. There are plans to make an [[archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:前方後円墳]]

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1996.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138687|title=和田岡古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The Wadaoka Kofun group is located in an area measuring approximately 2.5 kilometers north-south by one kilometer east-west, on a low plateau overlooking the Haranoya River west of the downtown area of modern Kakegawa city. The site consists of four [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}), 15 {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), and four {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped ''kofun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), all dating from the 5th century to the sixth century AD. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The largest include: {| class="wikitable" |+ Wadaoka Kofun group |- ! name !! The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. [[Grave goods]] recovered from the tombs included, large numbers of iron weapons, fragments of armor, pottery shards and ''[[shinju-kyo]]'' [[bronze mirrors]]. The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s [[Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|Hosoya Station]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1996.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138687|title=和田岡古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The Wadaoka Kofun group is located in an area measuring approximately 2.5 kilometers north-south by one kilometer east-west, on a low plateau overlooking the Haranoya River west of the downtown area of modern Kakegawa city. The site consists of four [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}), 15 {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), and four {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped ''kofun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), all dating from the 5th century to the sixth century AD. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The largest include: {| class="wikitable" |+ Wadaoka Kofun group |- ! name !! Type !! Length |- | Wakanezuka Kofun
各和金塚古墳 || keyhole || 66.4 meters
Anterior: 20.5 m wide x 4 m high: Posterior 51.2 m diameter x 6.5 m high |- | Hisagozuka Kofun
瓢塚古墳 || keyhole || 63 meters long
Anterior: 25.2 m wide x 3.5 m high: Posterior 37.8 m diameter x 3.5 m high |- | Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun
吉岡大塚古墳|| keyhole || 55 meters long
Anterior: 27.5 m wide x 2.5 m high: Posterior 41.3 m diameter x 7.2 m high |- | Gyojinzuka Kofun
行人塚古墳 || keyhole || 43.7 meters long
Anterior: 16 m wide: Posterior 25.4 m diameter |- | Harubayashiin Kofun
春林院古墳 || circular || 35 meters, height 5 meters |- | Usa-Hachiman-sha Kofun || circular || 30 meter diameter |- | Gongenyama Kofun || circular || 40 meter diameter |- | Ishinogata Kofun || circular || 25 meter diameter |- |} These tumuli are believed to be the tombs of local kings who ruled the Haranodani River basin. The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. [[Grave goods]] recovered from the tombs included, large numbers of iron weapons, fragments of armor, pottery shards and ''[[shinju-kyo]]'' [[bronze mirrors]]. The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s [[Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|Hosoya Station]]. There are plans to make an [[archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:前方後円墳]]

There are plans to make an [[archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:前方後円墳]]

{{Short description|Archaeological site in the Tōkai region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Wadaoka Kofun group | native_name = 和田岡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Wadaoka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|40|N|137|57|06|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kakegawa, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Wadaoka Kofun group'''|和田岡古墳群|Wadaoka kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] with a cluster of [[burial mound]]s from the [[Kofun period]] located in what is now part of the city of [[Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1996.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138687|title=和田岡古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The Wadaoka Kofun group is located in an area measuring approximately 2.5 kilometers north-south by one kilometer east-west, on a low plateau overlooking the Haranoya River west of the downtown area of modern Kakegawa city. The site consists of four [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), 15 {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), and four {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped ''kofun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), all dating from the 5th century to the sixth century AD. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The largest include: {| class="wikitable" |+ Wadaoka Kofun group |- ! name !! type !! Length |- | Wakanezuka Kofun
各和金塚古墳 || keyhole || 66.4 meters
Anterior: 20.5 m wide x 4 m high: Posterior 51.2 m diameter x 6.5 m high |- | Hisagozuka Kofun
瓢塚古墳 || keyhole || 63 meters long
Anterior: 25.2 m wide x 3.5 m high: Posterior 37.8 m diameter x 3.5 m high |- | Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun
吉岡大塚古墳|| keyhole || 55 meters long
Anterior: 27.5 m wide x 2.5 m high: Posterior 41.3 m diameter x 7.2 m high |- | Gyojinzuka Kofun
行人塚古墳 || keyhole || 43.7 meters long
Anterior: 16 m wide: Posterior 25.4 m diameter |- | Harubayashiin Kofun
春林院古墳 || circular || 35 meters, height 5 meters |- | Usa-Hachiman-sha Kofun || circular || 30 meter diameter |- | Gongenyama Kofun || circular || 40 meter diameter |- | Ishinogata Kofun || circular || 25 meter diameter |- |} These tumuli are believed to be the tombs of local kings who ruled the Haranodani River basin. The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. [[Grave goods]] recovered from the tombs included, large numbers of iron weapons, fragments of armor, pottery shards and ''[[shinju-kyo]]'' [[bronze mirrors]]. The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s [[Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|Hosoya Station]]. There are plans to make an [[archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:円墳]] [[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]]

The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. 春林院古墳 || circular || 35 meters, height 5 meters |- | Usa-Hachiman-sha Kofun || circular || 30 meter diameter |- | Gongenyama Kofun || circular || 40 meter diameter |- | Ishinogata Kofun || circular || 25 meter diameter |- |} These tumuli are believed to be the tombs of local kings who ruled the Haranodani River basin. The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. [[Grave goods]] recovered from the tombs included, large numbers of iron weapons, fragments of armor, pottery shards and ''[[shinju-kyo]]'' [[bronze mirrors]]. The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s [[Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|Hosoya Station]]. There are plans to make an [[archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:11]]

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1996.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138687|title=和田岡古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The Wadaoka Kofun group is located in an area measuring approximately 2.5 kilometers north-south by one kilometer east-west, on a low plateau overlooking the Haranoya River west of the downtown area of modern Kakegawa city. The site consists of four [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), 15 {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), and four {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped ''kofun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), all dating from the 5th century to the sixth century AD. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The largest include: {| class="wikitable" |+ Wadaoka Kofun group |- ! name !! type !! There are plans to make an [[archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{Short description|Archaeological site in the Tōkai region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Wadaoka Kofun group | native_name = 和田岡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Wadaoka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|40|N|137|57|06|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kakegawa, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | Group = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Wadaoka Kofun group'''|和田岡古墳群|Wadaoka kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] with a cluster of [[Kofun]]s from the [[Kofun period]] located in what is now part of the city of [[Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1996.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138687|title=和田岡古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The Wadaoka Kofun group is located in an area measuring approximately 2.5 kilometers north-south by one kilometer east-west, on a low plateau overlooking the Haranoya River west of the downtown area of modern Kakegawa city. The site consists of four [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), 15 {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), and four {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped ''kofun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), all dating from the 5th century to the sixth century AD. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The largest include: {| class="wikitable" |+ Wadaoka Kofun group |- ! name !! type !! Length |- | Wakanezuka Kofun
各和金塚古墳 || keyhole || 66.4 meters
Anterior: 20.5 m wide x 4 m high: Posterior 51.2 m diameter x 6.5 m high |- | Hisagozuka Kofun
瓢塚古墳 || keyhole || 63 meters long
Anterior: 25.2 m wide x 3.5 m high: Posterior 37.8 m diameter x 3.5 m high |- | Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun
吉岡大塚古墳|| keyhole || 55 meters long
Anterior: 27.5 m wide x 2.5 m high: Posterior 41.3 m diameter x 7.2 m high |- | Gyojinzuka Kofun
行人塚古墳 || keyhole || 43.7 meters long
Anterior: 16 m wide: Posterior 25.4 m diameter |- | Harubayashiin Kofun
春林院古墳 || circular || 35 meters, height 5 meters |- | Usa-Hachiman-sha Kofun || circular || 30 meter diameter |- | Gongenyama Kofun || circular || 40 meter diameter |- | Ishinogata Kofun || circular || 25 meter diameter |- |} These tumuli are believed to be the tombs of local kings who ruled the Haranodani River basin. The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. [[Grave goods]] recovered from the tombs included, large numbers of iron weapons, fragments of armor, pottery shards and ''[[shinju-kyo]]'' [[bronze mirrors]]. The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s [[Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|Hosoya Station]]. There are plans to make an [[archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}}

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1996.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138687|title=和田岡古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The Wadaoka Kofun group is located in an area measuring approximately 2.5 kilometers north-south by one kilometer east-west, on a low plateau overlooking the Haranoya River west of the downtown area of modern Kakegawa city. The site consists of four [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), 15 {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), and four {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped ''kofun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), all dating from the 5th century to the sixth century AD. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The largest include: {| class="wikitable" |+ Wadaoka Kofun group |- ! name !! type !! The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. [[Grave goods]] recovered from the tombs included, large numbers of iron weapons, fragments of armor, pottery shards and ''[[shinju-kyo]]'' [[bronze mirrors]]. The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s [[Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|Hosoya Station]]. There are plans to make an [[archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}}

{{Short description|Archaeological site in the Tōkai region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Wadaoka Kofun group | native_name = 和田岡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Wadaoka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|40|N|137|57|06|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kakegawa, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Wadaoka Kofun group'''|和田岡古墳群|Wadaoka kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] with a cluster of [[Kofun]]s from the [[Kofun period]] located in what is now part of the city of [[Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1996.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138687|title=和田岡古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The Wadaoka Kofun group is located in an area measuring approximately 2.5 kilometers north-south by one kilometer east-west, on a low plateau overlooking the Haranoya River west of the downtown area of modern Kakegawa city. The site consists of four [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), 15 {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), and four {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped ''kofun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), all dating from the 5th century to the sixth century AD. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The largest include: {| class="wikitable" |+ Wadaoka Kofun group |- ! name !! type !! Length |- | Wakanezuka Kofun
各和金塚古墳 || keyhole || 66.4 meters
Anterior: 20.5 m wide x 4 m high: Posterior 51.2 m diameter x 6.5 m high |- | Hisagozuka Kofun
瓢塚古墳 || keyhole || 63 meters long
Anterior: 25.2 m wide x 3.5 m high: Posterior 37.8 m diameter x 3.5 m high |- | Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun
吉岡大塚古墳|| keyhole || 55 meters long
Anterior: 27.5 m wide x 2.5 m high: Posterior 41.3 m diameter x 7.2 m high |- | Gyojinzuka Kofun
行人塚古墳 || keyhole || 43.7 meters long
Anterior: 16 m wide: Posterior 25.4 m diameter |- | Harubayashiin Kofun
春林院古墳 || circular || 35 meters, height 5 meters |- | Usa-Hachiman-sha Kofun || circular || 30 meter diameter |- | Gongenyama Kofun || circular || 40 meter diameter |- | Ishinogata Kofun || circular || 25 meter diameter |- |} These tumuli are believed to be the tombs of local kings who ruled the Haranodani River basin. The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. [[Grave goods]] recovered from the tombs included, large numbers of iron weapons, fragments of armor, pottery shards and ''[[shinju-kyo]]'' [[bronze mirrors]]. The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s [[Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|Hosoya Station]]. There are plans to make an [[archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:kk]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]]

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1996.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138687|title=和田岡古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The Wadaoka Kofun group is located in an area measuring approximately 2.5 kilometers north-south by one kilometer east-west, on a low plateau overlooking the Haranoya River west of the downtown area of modern Kakegawa city. The site consists of four [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), 15 {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), and four {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped ''kofun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), all dating from the 5th century to the sixth century AD. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The largest include: {| class="wikitable" |+ Wadaoka Kofun group |- ! name !! type !! Length |- | Wakanezuka Kofun
各和金塚古墳 || keyhole || 66.4 meters
Anterior: 20.5 m wide x 4 m high: Posterior 51.2 m diameter x 6.5 m high |- | Hisagozuka Kofun
瓢塚古墳 || keyhole || 63 meters long
Anterior: 25.2 m wide x 3.5 m high: Posterior 37.8 m diameter x 3.5 m high |- | Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun
吉岡大塚古墳|| keyhole || 55 meters long
Anterior: 27.5 m wide x 2.5 m high: Posterior 41.3 m diameter x 7.2 m high |- | Gyojinzuka Kofun
行人塚古墳 || keyhole || 43.7 meters long
Anterior: 16 m wide: Posterior 25.4 m diameter |- | Harubayashiin Kofun
春林院古墳 || circular || 35 meters, height 5 meters |- | Usa-Hachiman-sha Kofun || circular || 30 meter diameter |- | Gongenyama Kofun || circular || 40 meter diameter |- | Ishinogata Kofun || circular || 25 meter diameter |- |} These tumuli are believed to be the tombs of local kings who ruled the Haranodani River basin. The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. [[Grave goods]] recovered from the tombs included, large numbers of iron weapons, fragments of armor, pottery shards and ''[[shinju-kyo]]'' [[bronze mirrors]]. The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s [[Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|Hosoya Station]]. There are plans to make an [[archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:kk]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]]

Anterior: 27.5 m wide x 2.5 m high: Posterior 41.3 m diameter x 7.2 m high |- | Gyojinzuka Kofun

The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. [[Grave goods]] recovered from the tombs included, large numbers of iron weapons, fragments of armor, pottery shards and ''[[shinju-kyo]]'' [[bronze mirrors]]. The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s [[Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|Hosoya Station]]. {{Short description|Archaeological site in the Tōkai region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Wadaoka Kofun group | native_name = 和田岡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Wadaoka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|40|N|137|57|06|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kakegawa, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Wadaoka Kofun group'''|和田岡古墳群|Wadaoka kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] with a cluster of [[Kofun]]s from the [[Kofun period]] located in what is now part of the city of [[Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1996.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138687|title=和田岡古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The Wadaoka Kofun group is located in an area measuring approximately 2.5 kilometers north-south by one kilometer east-west, on a low plateau overlooking the Haranoya River west of the downtown area of modern Kakegawa city. The site consists of four [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), 15 {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), and four {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped ''kofun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), all dating from the 5th century to the sixth century AD. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The largest include: {| class="wikitable" |+ Wadaoka Kofun group |- ! name !! type !! Length |- | Wakanezuka Kofun
各和金塚古墳 || keyhole || 66.4 meters
Anterior: 20.5 m wide x 4 m high: Posterior 51.2 m diameter x 6.5 m high |- | Hisagozuka Kofun
瓢塚古墳 || keyhole || 63 meters long
Anterior: 25.2 m wide x 3.5 m high: Posterior 37.8 m diameter x 3.5 m high |- | Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun
吉岡大塚古墳|| keyhole || 55 meters long
Anterior: 27.5 m wide x 2.5 m high: Posterior 41.3 m diameter x 7.2 m high |- | Gyojinzuka Kofun
行人塚古墳 || keyhole || 43.7 meters long
Anterior: 16 m wide: Posterior 25.4 m diameter |- | Harubayashiin Kofun
春林院古墳 || circular || 35 meters, height 5 meters |- | Usa-Hachiman-sha Kofun || circular || 30 meter diameter |- | Gongenyama Kofun || circular || 40 meter diameter |- | Ishinogata Kofun || circular || 25 meter diameter |- |} These tumuli are believed to be the tombs of local kings who ruled the Haranodani River basin. The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. [[Grave goods]] recovered from the tombs included, large numbers of iron weapons, fragments of armor, pottery shards and ''[[shinju-kyo]]'' [[bronze mirrors]]. The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s [[Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|Hosoya Station]]. There are plans to make an [[archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] {{Kofun navbar}}

The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. [[Grave goods]] recovered from the tombs included, large numbers of iron weapons, fragments of armor, pottery shards and ''[[shinju-kyo]]'' [[bronze mirrors]]. The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s [[Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|Hosoya Station]]. There are plans to make an [[archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Short description|Archaeological site in the Tōkai region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Wadaoka Kofun group | native_name = 和田岡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Wadaoka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|40|N|137|57|06|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kakegawa, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Wadaoka Kofun group'''|和田岡古墳群|Wadaoka kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] with a cluster of [[Kofun]]s from the [[Kofun period]] located in what is now part of the city of [[Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1996.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138687|title=和田岡古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The Wadaoka Kofun group is located in an area measuring approximately 2.5 kilometers north-south by one kilometer east-west, on a low plateau overlooking the Haranoya River west of the downtown area of modern Kakegawa city. The site consists of four [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), 15 {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), and four {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped ''kofun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), all dating from the 5th century to the sixth century AD. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The largest include: {| class="wikitable" |+ Wadaoka Kofun group |- ! name !! type !! The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. [[Grave goods]] recovered from the tombs included, large numbers of iron weapons, fragments of armor, pottery shards and ''[[shinju-kyo]]'' [[bronze mirrors]]. The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s [[Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|Hosoya Station]]. There are plans to make an [[archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Wadaoka Kofun group]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] {{Kofun navbar}}
