The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1996.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138687|title=和田岡古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The Wadaoka Kofun group is located in an area measuring approximately 2.5 kilometers north-south by one kilometer east-west, on a low plateau overlooking the Haranoya River west of the downtown area of modern Kakegawa city. The site consists of four [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}), 15 {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), and four {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped ''kofun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), all dating from the 5th century to the sixth century AD. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The largest include: {| class="wikitable" |+ Wadaoka Kofun group |- ! name !! type !! Length |- | Wakanezuka Kofun
各和金塚古墳 || keyhole || 66.4 meters
Anterior: 20.5 m wide x 4 m high: Posterior 51.2 m diameter x 6.5 m high |- | Hisagozuka Kofun
瓢塚古墳 || keyhole || 63 meters long
Anterior: 25.2 m wide x 3.5 m high: Posterior 37.8 m diameter x 3.5 m high |- | Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun
吉岡大塚古墳|| keyhole || 55 meters long
Anterior: 27.5 m wide x 2.5 m high: Posterior 41.3 m diameter x 7.2 m high |- | Gyojinzuka Kofun
行人塚古墳 || keyhole || 43.7 meters long
Anterior: 16 m wide: Posterior 25.4 m diameter |- | Harubayashiin Kofun
春林院古墳 || circular || 35 meters, height 5 meters |- | Usa-Hachiman-sha Kofun || circular || 30 meter diameter |- | Gongenyama Kofun || circular || 40 meter diameter |- | Ishinogata Kofun || circular || 25 meter diameter |- |} These tumuli are believed to be the tombs of local kings who ruled the Haranodani River basin. The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] recovered from the tombs included, large numbers of iron weapons, fragments of armor, pottery shards and ''[[shinju-kyo]]'' [[:en:Bronze mirrors|Bronze mirrors]]. The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[:en:Tenryu Hamanako Line|Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s [[:en:Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|Hosoya Station]]. There are plans to make an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] {{wikidata link|Q85814111}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q85814111}}

[[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] {{Short description|Archaeological site in the Tōkai region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Wadaoka Kofun group | native_name = 和田岡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Wadaoka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|40|N|137|57|06|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kakegawa, Shizuoka|Kakegawa, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Wadaoka Kofun group'''|和田岡古墳群|Wadaoka kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] with a cluster of [[Kofun]]s from the [[Kofun period]] located in what is now part of the city of [[:en:Kakegawa, Shizuoka|Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] in the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. [[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] {{Short description|Archaeological site in the Tōkai region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Wadaoka Kofun group | native_name = 和田岡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Wadaoka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|40|N|137|57|06|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kakegawa, Shizuoka|Kakegawa, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Wadaoka Kofun group'''|和田岡古墳群|Wadaoka kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] with a cluster of [[Kofun]]s from the [[Kofun period]] located in what is now part of the city of [[:en:Kakegawa, Shizuoka|Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] in the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1996.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138687|title=和田岡古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The Wadaoka Kofun group is located in an area measuring approximately 2.5 kilometers north-south by one kilometer east-west, on a low plateau overlooking the Haranoya River west of the downtown area of modern Kakegawa city. The site consists of four [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}), 15 {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), and four {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped ''kofun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), all dating from the 5th century to the sixth century AD. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The largest include: {| class="wikitable" |+ Wadaoka Kofun group |- ! name !! type !! Length |- | Wakanezuka Kofun
各和金塚古墳 || keyhole || 66.4 meters
Anterior: 20.5 m wide x 4 m high: Posterior 51.2 m diameter x 6.5 m high |- | Hisagozuka Kofun
瓢塚古墳 || keyhole || 63 meters long
Anterior: 25.2 m wide x 3.5 m high: Posterior 37.8 m diameter x 3.5 m high |- | Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun
吉岡大塚古墳|| keyhole || 55 meters long
Anterior: 27.5 m wide x 2.5 m high: Posterior 41.3 m diameter x 7.2 m high |- | Gyojinzuka Kofun
行人塚古墳 || keyhole || 43.7 meters long
Anterior: 16 m wide: Posterior 25.4 m diameter |- | Harubayashiin Kofun
春林院古墳 || circular || 35 meters, height 5 meters |- | Usa-Hachiman-sha Kofun || circular || 30 meter diameter |- | Gongenyama Kofun || circular || 40 meter diameter |- | Ishinogata Kofun || circular || 25 meter diameter |- |} These tumuli are believed to be the tombs of local kings who ruled the Haranodani River basin. The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] recovered from the tombs included, large numbers of iron weapons, fragments of armor, pottery shards and ''[[shinju-kyo]]'' [[:en:Bronze mirrors|Bronze mirrors]]. The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[:en:Tenryu Hamanako Line|Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s [[:en:Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|Hosoya Station]]. There are plans to make an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q85814111}}

The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[:en:Tenryu Hamanako Line|Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s [[:en:Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|Hosoya Station]]. There are plans to make an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] {{wikidata link|Q85814111}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] {{Short description|Archaeological site in the Tōkai region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Wadaoka Kofun group | native_name = 和田岡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Wadaoka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|40|N|137|57|06|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kakegawa, Shizuoka|Kakegawa, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Wadaoka Kofun group'''|和田岡古墳群|Wadaoka kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] with a cluster of [[Kofun]]s from the [[Kofun period]] located in what is now part of the city of [[:en:Kakegawa, Shizuoka|Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] in the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1996.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138687|title=和田岡古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The Wadaoka Kofun group is located in an area measuring approximately 2.5 kilometers north-south by one kilometer east-west, on a low plateau overlooking the Haranoya River west of the downtown area of modern Kakegawa city. The site consists of four [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}), 15 {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), and four {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped ''kofun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), all dating from the 5th century to the sixth century AD. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The largest include: {| class="wikitable" |+ Wadaoka Kofun group |- ! name !! type !! Length |- | Wakanezuka Kofun
各和金塚古墳 || keyhole || 66.4 meters
Anterior: 20.5 m wide x 4 m high: Posterior 51.2 m diameter x 6.5 m high |- | Hisagozuka Kofun
瓢塚古墳 || keyhole || 63 meters long
Anterior: 25.2 m wide x 3.5 m high: Posterior 37.8 m diameter x 3.5 m high |- | Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun
吉岡大塚古墳|| keyhole || 55 meters long
Anterior: 27.5 m wide x 2.5 m high: Posterior 41.3 m diameter x 7.2 m high |- | Gyojinzuka Kofun
行人塚古墳 || keyhole || 43.7 meters long
Anterior: 16 m wide: Posterior 25.4 m diameter |- | Harubayashiin Kofun
春林院古墳 || circular || 35 meters, height 5 meters |- | Usa-Hachiman-sha Kofun || circular || 30 meter diameter |- | Gongenyama Kofun || circular || 40 meter diameter |- | Ishinogata Kofun || circular || 25 meter diameter |- |} These tumuli are believed to be the tombs of local kings who ruled the Haranodani River basin. The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] recovered from the tombs included, large numbers of iron weapons, fragments of armor, pottery shards and ''[[shinju-kyo]]'' [[:en:Bronze mirrors|Bronze mirrors]]. The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[:en:Tenryu Hamanako Line|Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s [[:en:Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|Hosoya Station]]. There are plans to make an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] {{wikidata link|Q85814111}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

春林院古墳 || circular || 35 meters, height 5 meters |- | Usa-Hachiman-sha Kofun || circular || 30 meter diameter |- | Gongenyama Kofun || circular || 40 meter diameter |- | Ishinogata Kofun || circular || 25 meter diameter |- |} These tumuli are believed to be the tombs of local kings who ruled the Haranodani River basin. The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} recovered from the tombs included, large numbers of iron weapons, fragments of armor, pottery shards and ''[[shinju-kyo]]'' [[:en:Bronze mirrors|Bronze mirrors]]. The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[:en:Tenryu Hamanako Line|Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s {{ill|Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|en|Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|WD=Q5908235|lt=Hosoya Station}}. There are plans to make an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|WD=Q6573285|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] {{wikidata link|Q85814111}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] {{Short description|Archaeological site in the Tōkai region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Wadaoka Kofun group | native_name = 和田岡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Wadaoka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|40|N|137|57|06|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|WD=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Wadaoka Kofun group'''|和田岡古墳群|Wadaoka kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] with a cluster of [[Kofun]]s from the [[Kofun period]] located in what is now part of the city of {{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|WD=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}} in the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1996.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138687|title=和田岡古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The Wadaoka Kofun group is located in an area measuring approximately 2.5 kilometers north-south by one kilometer east-west, on a low plateau overlooking the Haranoya River west of the downtown area of modern Kakegawa city. The site consists of four {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped ''kofun''}} ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}), 15 {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), and four {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped ''kofun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), all dating from the 5th century to the sixth century AD. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The largest include: {| class="wikitable" |+ Wadaoka Kofun group |- ! name !! type !! The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} recovered from the tombs included, large numbers of iron weapons, fragments of armor, pottery shards and ''[[shinju-kyo]]'' [[:en:Bronze mirrors|Bronze mirrors]]. The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[:en:Tenryu Hamanako Line|Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s {{ill|Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|en|Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|WD=Q5908235|lt=Hosoya Station}}. There are plans to make an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|WD=Q6573285|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] {{wikidata link|Q85814111}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1996.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138687|title=和田岡古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The Wadaoka Kofun group is located in an area measuring approximately 2.5 kilometers north-south by one kilometer east-west, on a low plateau overlooking the Haranoya River west of the downtown area of modern Kakegawa city. The site consists of four {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped ''kofun''}} ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}), 15 {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), and four {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped ''kofun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), all dating from the 5th century to the sixth century AD. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The largest include: {| class="wikitable" |+ Wadaoka Kofun group |- ! name !! type !! Length |- | Wakanezuka Kofun
各和金塚古墳 || keyhole || 66.4 meters
Anterior: 20.5 m wide x 4 m high: Posterior 51.2 m diameter x 6.5 m high |- | Hisagozuka Kofun
瓢塚古墳 || keyhole || 63 meters long
Anterior: 25.2 m wide x 3.5 m high: Posterior 37.8 m diameter x 3.5 m high |- | Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun
吉岡大塚古墳|| keyhole || 55 meters long
Anterior: 27.5 m wide x 2.5 m high: Posterior 41.3 m diameter x 7.2 m high |- | Gyojinzuka Kofun
行人塚古墳 || keyhole || 43.7 meters long
Anterior: 16 m wide: Posterior 25.4 m diameter |- | Harubayashiin Kofun
春林院古墳 || circular || 35 meters, height 5 meters |- | Usa-Hachiman-sha Kofun || circular || 30 meter diameter |- | Gongenyama Kofun || circular || 40 meter diameter |- | Ishinogata Kofun || circular || 25 meter diameter |- |} These tumuli are believed to be the tombs of local kings who ruled the Haranodani River basin. The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} recovered from the tombs included, large numbers of iron weapons, fragments of armor, pottery shards and ''[[shinju-kyo]]'' [[:en:Bronze mirrors|Bronze mirrors]]. The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[:en:Tenryu Hamanako Line|Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s {{ill|Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|en|Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|WD=Q5908235|lt=Hosoya Station}}. There are plans to make an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|WD=Q6573285|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] {{wikidata link|Q85814111}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1996.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138687|title=和田岡古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The Wadaoka Kofun group is located in an area measuring approximately 2.5 kilometers north-south by one kilometer east-west, on a low plateau overlooking the Haranoya River west of the downtown area of modern Kakegawa city. The site consists of four {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped ''kofun''}} ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}), 15 {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), and four {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped ''kofun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), all dating from the 5th century to the sixth century AD. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The largest include: {| class="wikitable" |+ Wadaoka Kofun group |- ! name !! type !! The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. There are plans to make an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|WD=Q6573285|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] {{wikidata link|Q85814111}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

行人塚古墳 || keyhole || 43.7 meters long
Anterior: 16 m wide: Posterior 25.4 m diameter |- | Harubayashiin Kofun
春林院古墳 || circular || 35 meters, height 5 meters |- | Usa-Hachiman-sha Kofun || circular || 30 meter diameter |- | Gongenyama Kofun || circular || 40 meter diameter |- | Ishinogata Kofun || circular || 25 meter diameter |- |} These tumuli are believed to be the tombs of local kings who ruled the Haranodani River basin. The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} recovered from the tombs included, large numbers of iron weapons, fragments of armor, pottery shards and ''[[shinju-kyo]]'' [[:en:Bronze mirrors|Bronze mirrors]]. The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[:en:Tenryu Hamanako Line|Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s {{ill|Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|en|Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|WD=Q5908235|lt=Hosoya Station|1=Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)}}. There are plans to make an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|WD=Q6573285|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] {{wikidata link|Q85814111}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

[[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] {{Short description|Archaeological site in the Tōkai region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Wadaoka Kofun group | native_name = 和田岡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Wadaoka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|40|N|137|57|06|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|WD=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa, Shizuoka|1=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Wadaoka Kofun group'''|和田岡古墳群|Wadaoka kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] with a cluster of [[Kofun]]s from the [[Kofun period]] located in what is now part of the city of {{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|WD=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa, Shizuoka|1=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}} in the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1996.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138687|title=和田岡古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The Wadaoka Kofun group is located in an area measuring approximately 2.5 kilometers north-south by one kilometer east-west, on a low plateau overlooking the Haranoya River west of the downtown area of modern Kakegawa city. The site consists of four {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}), 15 {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), and four {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped ''kofun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), all dating from the 5th century to the sixth century AD. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The largest include: {| class="wikitable" |+ Wadaoka Kofun group |- ! name !! type !! The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[:en:Tenryu Hamanako Line|Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s {{ill|Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|en|Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|WD=Q5908235|lt=Hosoya Station|1=Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)}}. There are plans to make an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|WD=Q6573285|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] {{wikidata link|Q85814111}} [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

[[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] {{Short description|Archaeological site in the Tōkai region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Wadaoka Kofun group | native_name = 和田岡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Wadaoka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|40|N|137|57|06|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|WD=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa, Shizuoka|1=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Wadaoka Kofun group'''|和田岡古墳群|Wadaoka kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] with a cluster of [[Kofun]]s from the [[Kofun period]] located in what is now part of the city of {{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|WD=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa, Shizuoka|1=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}} in the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1996.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138687|title=和田岡古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The Wadaoka Kofun group is located in an area measuring approximately 2.5 kilometers north-south by one kilometer east-west, on a low plateau overlooking the Haranoya River west of the downtown area of modern Kakegawa city. The site consists of four {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}), 15 {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), and four {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped ''kofun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), all dating from the 5th century to the sixth century AD. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The largest include: {| class="wikitable" |+ Wadaoka Kofun group |- ! name !! type !! Length |- | Wakanezuka Kofun
各和金塚古墳 || keyhole || 66.4 meters
Anterior: 20.5 m wide x 4 m high: Posterior 51.2 m diameter x 6.5 m high |- | Hisagozuka Kofun
瓢塚古墳 || keyhole || 63 meters long
Anterior: 25.2 m wide x 3.5 m high: Posterior 37.8 m diameter x 3.5 m high |- | Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun
吉岡大塚古墳|| keyhole || 55 meters long
Anterior: 27.5 m wide x 2.5 m high: Posterior 41.3 m diameter x 7.2 m high |- | Gyojinzuka Kofun
行人塚古墳 || keyhole || 43.7 meters long
Anterior: 16 m wide: Posterior 25.4 m diameter |- | Harubayashiin Kofun
春林院古墳 || circular || 35 meters, height 5 meters |- | Usa-Hachiman-sha Kofun || circular || 30 meter diameter |- | Gongenyama Kofun || circular || 40 meter diameter |- | Ishinogata Kofun || circular || 25 meter diameter |- |} These tumuli are believed to be the tombs of local kings who ruled the Haranodani River basin. The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} recovered from the tombs included, large numbers of iron weapons, fragments of armor, pottery shards and ''[[shinju-kyo]]'' [[:en:Bronze mirrors|Bronze mirrors]]. The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[:en:Tenryu Hamanako Line|Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s {{ill|Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|en|Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|WD=Q5908235|lt=Hosoya Station|1=Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)}}. There are plans to make an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|WD=Q6573285|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] {{wikidata link|Q85814111}} [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] [[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] {{Short description|Archaeological site in the Tōkai region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Wadaoka Kofun group | native_name = 和田岡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Wadaoka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|40|N|137|57|06|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|WD=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa, Shizuoka|1=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Wadaoka Kofun group'''|和田岡古墳群|Wadaoka kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] with a cluster of [[Kofun]]s from the [[Kofun period]] located in what is now part of the city of {{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|WD=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa, Shizuoka|1=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}} in the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1996.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138687|title=和田岡古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The Wadaoka Kofun group is located in an area measuring approximately 2.5 kilometers north-south by one kilometer east-west, on a low plateau overlooking the Haranoya River west of the downtown area of modern Kakegawa city. The site consists of four {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|1=[[Zenpōkōenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}), 15 {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), and four {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped ''kofun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), all dating from the 5th century to the sixth century AD. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The largest include: {| class="wikitable" |+ Wadaoka Kofun group |- ! name !! type !! Length |- | Wakanezuka Kofun
各和金塚古墳 || keyhole || 66.4 meters
Anterior: 20.5 m wide x 4 m high: Posterior 51.2 m diameter x 6.5 m high |- | Hisagozuka Kofun
瓢塚古墳 || keyhole || 63 meters long
Anterior: 25.2 m wide x 3.5 m high: Posterior 37.8 m diameter x 3.5 m high |- | Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun
吉岡大塚古墳|| keyhole || 55 meters long
Anterior: 27.5 m wide x 2.5 m high: Posterior 41.3 m diameter x 7.2 m high |- | Gyojinzuka Kofun
行人塚古墳 || keyhole || 43.7 meters long
Anterior: 16 m wide: Posterior 25.4 m diameter |- | Harubayashiin Kofun
春林院古墳 || circular || 35 meters, height 5 meters |- | Usa-Hachiman-sha Kofun || circular || 30 meter diameter |- | Gongenyama Kofun || circular || 40 meter diameter |- | Ishinogata Kofun || circular || 25 meter diameter |- |} These tumuli are believed to be the tombs of local kings who ruled the Haranodani River basin. The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. There are plans to make an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|WD=Q6573285|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[en:Wadaoka Kofun group]] {{wikidata link|Q85814111}} [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{Short description|Archaeological site in the Tōkai region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Wadaoka Kofun group | native_name = 和田岡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun before renovation | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Wadaoka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|40|N|137|57|06|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|WD=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa, Shizuoka|1=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Wadaoka Kofun group'''|和田岡古墳群|Wadaoka kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] with a cluster of [[Kofun]]s from the [[Kofun period]] located in what is now part of the city of {{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|WD=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa, Shizuoka|1=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}} in the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1996.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138687|title=和田岡古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==Overview== The Wadaoka Kofun group is located in an area measuring approximately 2.5 kilometers north-south by one kilometer east-west, on a low plateau overlooking the Haranoya River west of the downtown area of modern Kakegawa city. The site consists of four {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}), 15 {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), and four {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped ''kofun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), all dating from the 5th century to the sixth century AD. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The largest include: {| class="wikitable" |+ Wadaoka Kofun group |- ! name !! type !! Length |- | Wakanezuka Kofun
各和金塚古墳 || keyhole || 66.4 meters
Anterior: 20.5 m wide x 4 m high: Posterior 51.2 m diameter x 6.5 m high |- | Hisagozuka Kofun
瓢塚古墳 || keyhole || 63 meters long
Anterior: 25.2 m wide x 3.5 m high: Posterior 37.8 m diameter x 3.5 m high |- | Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun
吉岡大塚古墳|| keyhole || 55 meters long
Anterior: 27.5 m wide x 2.5 m high: Posterior 41.3 m diameter x 7.2 m high |- | Gyojinzuka Kofun
行人塚古墳 || keyhole || 43.7 meters long
Anterior: 16 m wide: Posterior 25.4 m diameter |- | Harubayashiin Kofun
春林院古墳 || circular || 35 meters, height 5 meters |- | Usa-Hachiman-sha Kofun || circular || 30 meter diameter |- | Gongenyama Kofun || circular || 40 meter diameter |- | Ishinogata Kofun || circular || 25 meter diameter |- |} These tumuli are believed to be the tombs of local kings who ruled the Haranodani River basin. The Wakanezuka Kofun is considered the oldest, but its pit-type [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was robbed in 1974 before a proper [[archaeological excavation]] could be made. Many of the tumuli were once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' clay figurines, and the Yoshioka-Otsuka Kofun has traces of a moat remaining. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} recovered from the tombs included, large numbers of iron weapons, fragments of armor, pottery shards and ''[[shinju-kyo]]'' [[:en:Bronze mirrors|Bronze mirrors]]. The necropolis is located approximately 15 minutes on foot from the [[:en:Tenryu Hamanako Line|Tenryu Hamanako Line]]'s {{ill|Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|en|Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)|WD=Q5908235|lt=Hosoya Station|1=Hosoya Station (Shizuoka)}}. There are plans to make an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] in the area. ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|WD=Q6573285|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kankou/spot/rekishibunka/wadaokakofun.html Kakegawa home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{wikidata link|Q85814111|en|Wadaoka Kofun group}} [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
